name: Trade Ingestion + Validation
summary: Drop invalid rows, type-cast trade dates, regex-extract a commodity code, filter to current-year activity, and pivot a per-book exposure view from the nightly booking-system extract.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Nightly booking-system extract: WTI, Brent, Henry Hub, TTF, JKM, PJM trades.
# Schema: trade_id, trader_id, trade_date, value_date, settlement_date,
#         counterparty_id, instrument, commodity, product_type, notional, unit,
#         price, currency, buy_sell, book, portfolio, region, delivery_location, booked_at
trades = pd.read_csv("trades.csv")

# Booking systems sometimes emit half-written rows on overnight cutover.
trades = trades.dropna(subset=["trade_id", "trade_date", "notional"])

# Normalise the two date columns Risk uses for valuation.
trades["trade_date"] = pd.to_datetime(trades["trade_date"]).dt.date
trades["value_date"] = pd.to_datetime(trades["value_date"]).dt.date

# Pull the commodity prefix off the instrument string (e.g. WTI-DEC25 -> WTI).
trades["commodity_code"] = trades["instrument"].str.extract(r"^([A-Z]+)")

# Drop non-positive notionals and any future-dated rows.
filtered = trades[(trades["notional"] > 0) & (trades["trade_date"] <= "2026-04-26")]

# Per-book daily exposure across CRUDE / NATGAS / POWER.
exposure = filtered.pivot(index="book", columns="commodity", values="notional")
exposure.to_csv("trade_blotter.csv")

name: Trade Dedup Across Booking Systems
summary: Stack overlapping Endur, Allegro, and internal extracts during a migration window, dedup on (trade_id, version), keep the most recent booked copy, and emit a consolidated trade tape.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Three parallel booking systems during the Endur -> Allegro migration window.
# All three emit the canonical trade schema plus a `version` increment.
endur = pd.read_csv("endur_trades.csv")
allegro = pd.read_csv("allegro_trades.csv")
internal = pd.read_csv("internal_trades.csv")

# Stack the three extracts into a single tape.
combined = pd.concat([endur, allegro, internal])

# Dedup on (trade_id, version) so a version-stamped amendment is kept once.
combined = combined.drop_duplicates(subset=["trade_id", "version"])

# Most-recently booked copy wins any reporting tie.
combined = combined.sort_values("booked_at", ascending=False)

combined.to_csv("consolidated_trades.csv", index=False)

name: Book MtM End-of-Day
summary: Filter trades to current-year activity, join against the latest forward curve on (commodity, tenor, delivery_location), aggregate exposure by book and tenor, and rank the top 50 books for the EOD desk pack.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Booking-system trade extract and the latest forward-curve snapshot.
trades = pd.read_csv("trades.csv")
recent = trades[trades["trade_date"] >= "2024-01-01"]

# curves.csv schema: as_of_date, commodity, tenor, delivery_location,
#                    currency, mid_price, bid, ask, forward_date
curves = pd.read_csv("curves.csv")

# Multi-key join — the canonical price-tape lookup.
priced = recent.merge(
    curves,
    on=["commodity", "tenor", "delivery_location"],
    how="left",
)

# Roll up exposure to (book, commodity, tenor).
exposures = priced.groupby(["book", "commodity", "tenor"]).agg({
    "notional": "sum",
    "mid_price": "mean",
}).reset_index()

# Sort then rank the 50 largest books for the EOD desk pack.
exposures_sorted = exposures.sort_values(["book", "commodity"])
top_books = exposures_sorted.nlargest(50, "notional")
top_books.to_csv("book_mtm_eod.csv", index=False)

name: PJM LMP Tick Analytics
summary: Sort 5-minute LMP ticks per node, then compute a 6-tick (30-min) VWAP proxy and a 12-tick (1-hr) rolling volatility per pricing node — the canonical real-time WINDOW shape that surfaces the partition/order gap.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# 5-minute PJM LMP feed.
# Schema: iso, node_id, node_name, zone, timestamp,
#         lmp, congestion, losses, energy_component
ticks = pd.read_csv("pjm_lmps.csv")

# Sort by (node_id, timestamp) so per-node windows are well-defined.
ticks_sorted = ticks.sort_values(["node_id", "timestamp"])

# 30-minute rolling VWAP proxy per node.
ticks_vwap = (
    ticks_sorted.groupby("node_id")["lmp"]
    .rolling(6, on="timestamp")
    .mean()
    .reset_index()
    .rename(columns={"lmp": "vwap_30min"})
)

# 1-hour rolling volatility per node.
ticks_vol = (
    ticks_sorted.groupby("node_id")["lmp"]
    .rolling(12, on="timestamp")
    .std()
    .reset_index()
    .rename(columns={"lmp": "rolling_vol"})
)
ticks_vol.to_csv("pjm_tick_analytics.csv", index=False)

name: Forward Curve SCD Router
summary: Slowly-changing-dimension router: given a reference date, split a forward-curve history table into current and historical rows. The canonical cond/~cond shape that consolidates into a single 1-to-2 SPLIT recipe.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Forward-curve history: each row is a (commodity, tenor, delivery_location)
# quote with a validity window. effective_date <= ref_date < end_date means
# the quote is current; everything else is historical.
ref_date = "2026-04-26"
df = pd.read_csv("curve_history.csv")

# Bind the predicate so py-iku consolidates df[cond] / df[~cond] into ONE
# 1-to-2 SPLIT recipe rather than two single-output filters.
cond = (df["effective_date"] <= ref_date) & (
    (df["end_date"].isna()) | (df["end_date"] > ref_date)
)
current = df[cond]
history = df[~cond]

current.to_parquet("curves_current.parquet")
history.to_parquet("curves_history.parquet")

name: Mark Validation vs Broker
summary: Join internal mid marks against an external broker-quote feed on (commodity, tenor, delivery_location), drop nulls, rename to internal_mid / broker_mid, and gate downstream rows for IPV review.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Internal forward curves vs the broker-feed quote tape.
internal = pd.read_csv("curves.csv")
broker = pd.read_csv("broker_marks.csv")

# Inner-join the two snapshots on the canonical curve key.
merged = internal.merge(
    broker,
    on=["commodity", "tenor", "delivery_location"],
    how="inner",
    suffixes=("_int", "_brk"),
)

# Drop rows where either side is missing a mid price.
merged = merged.dropna(subset=["mid_price_int", "mid_price_brk"])

# Rename to the desk-readable column names IPV reviewers expect.
merged = merged.rename(columns={
    "mid_price_int": "internal_mid",
    "mid_price_brk": "broker_mid",
})

# Gate to non-negative internal marks before the IPV review queue.
flagged = merged[merged["internal_mid"] >= 0]
flagged.to_csv("mark_validation.csv", index=False)

name: Counterparty Default Features
summary: Build a counterparty-default feature set: join trades to the counterparty master, derive days_since_last_trade as a GREL formula, fill missing credit ratings with NR, qcut-bin current_exposure into Q1..Q5, one-hot encode credit_rating, drop nulls.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Trade ledger and counterparty master.
# counterparty_master.csv: counterparty_id, name, country, credit_rating,
#   exposure_limit, current_exposure, master_agreement (ISDA / NAESB / EFET / GTMA),
#   last_review_date, watch_list_flag
trades = pd.read_csv("trades.csv")
counterparties = pd.read_csv("counterparty_master.csv")
features = trades.merge(counterparties, on="counterparty_id", how="left")

# Days since the last trade against today's reference date.
features = features.assign(
    days_since_last_trade=lambda x: x["trade_date"] - "2026-04-26",
)

# 'NR' is the standard not-rated code.
features["credit_rating"] = features["credit_rating"].fillna("NR")

# Quintile bucket on current exposure.
features["exposure_bucket"] = pd.qcut(
    features["current_exposure"],
    q=5,
    labels=["Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4", "Q5"],
)

# One-hot encode the credit rating.
features = pd.get_dummies(features, columns=["credit_rating"])

# Drop rows with no recent activity signal.
features = features.dropna(subset=["days_since_last_trade"])
features.to_parquet("counterparty_features.parquet")

name: Counterparty Exposure Roll-Up
summary: Join trades to the counterparty master, roll up notional and trade-count by (counterparty_id, master_agreement, credit_rating), then rank the 25 largest counterparty exposures for the credit committee.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Trades and the counterparty master share counterparty_id.
trades = pd.read_csv("trades.csv")
counterparties = pd.read_csv("counterparty_master.csv")

# Enrich each trade with its counterparty record.
joined = trades.merge(counterparties, on="counterparty_id", how="left")

# Roll up exposure by (counterparty, master agreement, rating bucket).
rollup = joined.groupby([
    "counterparty_id",
    "master_agreement",
    "credit_rating",
]).agg({
    "notional": "sum",
    "trade_id": "count",
}).reset_index()

# Top 25 counterparties by aggregate notional.
ranked = rollup.nlargest(25, "notional")
ranked.to_csv("counterparty_exposure_rollup.csv", index=False)

name: PJM Hub-Locational Analysis
summary: Join PJM locational positions to the node-to-zone reference table, then aggregate volume_mwh and position count by (zone, hour_ending) for the WESTERN HUB / AEP / DOM exposure dashboard.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# lmp_positions.csv: position_id, trader_id, trade_date, iso, node_id, node_name,
#   zone, volume_mwh, position_type (LONG/SHORT), source_lmp, dest_lmp,
#   delivery_date, hour_ending
positions = pd.read_csv("lmp_positions.csv")

# node_to_zone.csv: node_id, node_name, zone, lat, lon, voltage_kv, region
nodes = pd.read_csv("node_to_zone.csv")

# Enrich each locational position with its zone.
enriched = positions.merge(
    nodes,
    on="node_id",
    how="left",
)

# Roll up volume and position count by (zone, hour_ending).
# Common PJM zones in the data: WESTERN HUB, AEP, DOM, AECO, ComEd.
by_zone = enriched.groupby(["zone", "hour_ending"]).agg({
    "volume_mwh": "sum",
    "position_id": "count",
}).reset_index()
by_zone.to_csv("pjm_hub_locational.csv", index=False)

name: Trade-Event Aggregation per Trader
summary: Real-time trade-lifecycle event stream from Endur and Allegro: count distinct trades and total events per trader for the trading-floor activity dashboard.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# trade_events.csv: event_id, trade_id, trader_id, event_type
#   (BOOKED / AMENDED / CANCELLED / MATCHED / SETTLED), event_at,
#   source_system (Endur / Allegro / Internal / Exchange),
#   session_id, instrument, region
events = pd.read_csv("trade_events.csv")

# trade_id COUNTD = distinct trades touched per trader.
# event_id COUNT  = total event volume per trader.
per_trader = events.groupby("trader_id").agg({
    "trade_id": "nunique",
    "event_id": "count",
}).reset_index()
per_trader.to_csv("trade_event_per_trader.csv", index=False)
